vocab.mnemonic: Imagine a FANGED snake lunging IN to capture its prey.
Imagine a **slut** in a movie dramatically saying "The end!" as she **slams** a door shut.
Imagine a "rod" of advice, like a wise fisherman handing you a fishing rod to counsel you.
Imagine a DJ wearing a "skirt" while caring for animals — DJ+skirt = djurskötare.
Imagine a **gift** box that’s **poisonous**, so if you unwrap it, you’ll get sick—*giftig* sounds like “gift sick.”
Picture a snake hissing "VÄSA" as it warns you to "VÄ" (go away) and "SA" (say) nothing, or you'll get bit!
Imagine a "quiche" filled with gas that makes you choke—"kvävas" sounds like "quiche gas" and suffocates you at a fancy party.
Picture a monkey (apmär) climbing UP a tree, screaming "Hey, look at me!" to grab your attention.
Imagine a rocket **r**oaring into the s**ky**—then, with a loud "**M**" sound, it **de**parts—so "rymde" sounds like "roam the sky" when you've escaped.
Think of a Swedish king named "Kung" who sips "Cobra" soda while ruling his snake kingdom.
Imagine a snake **sliding** through the grass, but its tail gets caught in a **wreath** (a "sling"), forcing it to **wiggle and writhe** to get free.
Imagine a **lock** on a treasure chest, and you're playing a flute to **lure** a snake out from under it — "locka fram" sounds like "lock a from," tricking the lock's owner into opening it.
Imagine a "boat" (bot) racing to deliver a "medal" (medel) that cures your poison—your "boat-medal" is the ultimate antidote!
Picture a giant "QUEUE" (kvävs) of people so tightly packed that the person in front can't breathe, and she "suffocates."
Respekt sounds like "re-spect," so picture a polite ghost wearing spectacles who says "I respect you" before disappearing.
Imagine a jaguar chasing you as you yell "JAGADE!"—like "jaguar" mixed with "aided" by your own screams while being hunted.
Imagine mice wearing tiny "moose" antlers, and as they scurry, they squeak "möss, möss" instead of "mice."
Imagine "scrap" metal is "trash" — just drop the 'c' and you've got "skräp" for rubbish.
Imagine "who's he?" asking who owns the dog, and the stern-faced man replies "Husse!" — like "who's he?" but it's the male owner.
Imagine a glass container shattering as you yank off its lid, revealing a peppery hot ice cream wrapper inside.
Imagine a sad, soggy "soup" (sopp) spilling from a torn bag, so you cry "Åse!" (like "oh, say!") as you grab a new garbage bag.
Think of a plucky little bird who "plockar" up crumbs with its beak, collecting them one by one.
Imagine a "bad" pirate "plats" his treasure chest at the very spot where you swim—so you always remember "badplats" is the beach.
Picture a "pasta" chef in Sweden **adapting** a recipe so it's *tailored* for a giant, saying "**Anpassad** pasta!" (An-pah-sad).
Imagine a "bear" who "holds" onto a jar of honey and "retains" it—saying "bear-holder" for "behåller."
Imagine a **red** lifebuoy that just **saved** a swimmer—"räddat" sounds like "red-dat" is the thing that rescued them.
Imagine a beaver building a DAM across a stream to create many small PONDS, and as it works, it mutters "DAM-are" (dammar) to remind itself of the job.
Imagine a "slapper" (släpper) unclipping a cage door so a bird can "ut" (hoot like "out!") fly free.
Picture a DUMP truck loudly dumping a load of trash onto a lover's front lawn to abandon them.
Imagine a giant **eco**-friendly **system** where a Viking shouts, "Eko! Systemet!" to remind you that every sound echoes through nature.
Imagine a **furry** AND **grass** field that suddenly morphs into a desert — "furry-and-grass" sounds like "förändras," meaning the landscape **changes**.
Imagine a "fist" full of "lösa" (loose) sand where the fish should be—completely fishless!
"Omplacerat" sounds like "om-placed-a rat" — imagine finding a rat in your kitchen, so you calmly pick it up and relocate it to a new home outside!
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine a moose named Hörsy putting on huge headphones for a hearing test, screaming "HÖR-SEL-TEST!" at the top of her lungs.
Imagine you're at a rehearsal, and the director shouts "ÖVA!" so you quickly "owe" the band a perfect practice session.
Imagine the "in" side of a jar is "håll" (holding) the "content" — like a label inside a jar listing what's in there.
Imagine a "vent" in a submarine where you must wait for air pressure to equalize—"väntetiden" sounds like "vent-a-tide-in," the tide of waiting time before you can open the hatch.
Imagine a **stark** (strong) Viking lifting heavy weights to **up** his muscle power—"stärka upp" means to strengthen!
"Gårdsvärd" sounds like "guards-ward," so picture a stern guard in a ward full of tiny yards, sweeping each one with a janitor's broom.
Picture a "bear" (arbet) working at a "fairy" (förmedlingen) desk, handing out job applications with sparkly wings.
Imagine a "bee" bringing a tiny "drag" of honey to its hive as a welfare benefit for the colony.
Imagine a "here and" now landlord handing you a bill for the rent, shouting "HYR AN!" (sounds like "here and") as he points to the spot where you must pay.
Think of "lik" as "like" and "värdig" as "worthy," so something "like-worthy" is its "equivalent" in value.
Imagine a "hem" (home) stitched into the land like a giant quilt, where every thread says "this is my home country."

vocab.context: the initiative, the investment (in a project)
vocab.mnemonic: Imagine you're launching a rocket ship (a SATellite) and your boss yells "SATSningen!" as you push the big red button—it's your big initiative, so you mustn't SIT on it.
Imagine a **trap** door in the **house** that opens into a hidden stairwell.
"FASTen your seatbelt, the property company’s BOLAgement is rolling out a new high-rise!"
Imagine "arbetslöshet" as a huge, sad **lös** (loose) **hat** floating away from a job site because it has no work to hold it down.
Imagine an "out-building" (utbildning) where you go to get trained—and you have to "build" your brain out in that shed.

customArticles.title: Kvinna räddar över 400 guldfiskar från dammar
customArticles.summary: Cheyenne van Keeken has rescued over 400 goldfish from ponds in Gothenburg, Sweden. She criticizes people for dumping goldfish in ponds, which can disrupt local ecosystems.

content.text: Cheyenne van Keeken har räddat över 400 guldfiskar från dammar i Göteborg. Hon säger till P4 Göteborg att det är synd att folk släpper ut guldfiskar i dammar.
content.translation: Cheyenne van Keeken has rescued over 400 goldfish from ponds in Gothenburg. She tells P4 Gothenburg that it's a shame that people release goldfish into ponds.

content.text: – Bara den här sommaren har jag varit på sex olika ställen. Det är så tragiskt att folk dumpar dem och dumpar djur på det sättet.
content.translation: – Just this summer I have been to six different places. It's so tragic that people dump them and dump animals in that way.

content.text: Vem som har släppt ut guldfiskarna i dammarna vet man inte. Fredrik Larsson som jobbar på Länsstyrelsen i Västra Götaland säger att ekosystemet i dammarna, alltså vilka växter och djur som lever där, kan förändras av fiskarna.
content.translation: Who released the goldfish into the ponds is unknown. Fredrik Larsson, who works at the County Administrative Board of Västra Götaland, says that the ecosystem in the ponds, meaning which plants and animals live there, can be changed by the fish.

content.text: – Många dammar är naturligt fisklösa vilket innebär att släpper man då helt plötsligt i fisk i de dammarna så kommer man att ändra ekosystemet i den dammen.
content.translation: – Many ponds are naturally fishless, which means that if you suddenly introduce fish into those ponds, you will change the ecosystem in that pond.

content.text: Cheyenne van Keeken säger att det är svårt att få plats med alla guldfiskar hemma. Hon låter några av dem bo i badkaret. Hon säger att hon behåller några fiskar själv och lämnar några vidare till andra personer.
content.translation: Cheyenne van Keeken says it's difficult to find room for all the goldfish at home. She lets some of them live in the bathtub. She says she keeps some fish herself and passes others on to other people.

content.text: – Guldfiskarna som har varit väldigt fina har jag ju valt att behålla själv. Men de allra största så har jag ju på direkten omplacerat dem för det går inte att ha dem här hemma. Jag har inte plats för dem.
content.translation: – The goldfish that have been very nice I have chosen to keep myself. But the very largest ones I have immediately rehomed because it's not possible to have them here at home. I don't have room for them.

customArticles.title: Kungskobran på Skansenakvariet har hittats
customArticles.summary: The escaped king cobra at Skansenakvariet in Stockholm has been located inside a wall near its enclosure. Staff plan to cut a hole to capture it.

content.text: Djurskötare har hittat den giftiga ormen som rymde från sin bur i lördags. Ormen är kvar på djurparken Skansenakvariet i Stockholm. Ormen är en kungskobra. Den är två och en halv meter lång och mycket giftig.
content.translation: Animal keepers have found the poisonous snake that escaped from its cage last Saturday. The snake is still at the Skansen Aquarium zoo in Stockholm. The snake is a king cobra. It is two and a half meters long and very poisonous.

content.text: Ormen är inne i en vägg nära sin bur. Djurskötarna har sett ormen med en speciell liten kamera. De har också hört kobran väsa. Det säger Skansenakvariets ägare Jonas Wahlström till P1 morgon.
content.translation: The snake is inside a wall near its cage. The animal keepers have seen the snake with a special small camera. They have also heard the cobra hiss. That's what Skansen Aquarium's owner Jonas Wahlström says to P1 Morning.

content.text: – Vi såg honom och hörde honom till och med väsa.
content.translation: – We saw him and even heard him hiss.

content.text: Nu ska de som jobbar i djurparken göra ett hål i väggen och försöka fånga in kobran.
content.translation: Now the people who work at the zoo will make a hole in the wall and try to catch the cobra.

content.text: Kobran rymde från sin bur för nästan en vecka sedan. Händelsen har fått mycket uppmärksamhet både i Sverige och i andra länder. Flera medier i USA har till exempel rapporterat om ormen.
content.translation: The cobra escaped from its cage almost a week ago. The event has received a lot of attention both in Sweden and in other countries. Several media outlets in the USA, for example, have reported about the snake.

content.text: Kungskobran är världens längsta giftiga orm. Den kan bli fem och en halv meter lång. Om en människa blir biten av ormen måste hen få ett botemedel snabbt. Annars kan giftet döda personen inom en timme. Giftet gör att offret kvävs. Ormen i Stockholm har varit lös länge, men ingen har blivit skadad.
content.translation: The king cobra is the world's longest poisonous snake. It can become five and a half meters long. If a person is bitten by the snake, they must get an antidote quickly. Otherwise, the poison can kill the person within an hour. The poison causes the victim to suffocate. The snake in Stockholm has been loose for a long time, but no one has been injured.
